name: hypersync
documentation:
# hypersync-client-python
Python package for [Envio's](https://envio.dev/) HyperSync client written in Rust

## Setup

Recommeded to use a venv to install the package.

```bash
python -m venv .venv
```

Then activate the venv before use.
```bash
source .venv/bin/activate
```

Then istall the packages with pip.

```bash
pip install -e .
```

### Examples in `examples/`

There are a collection of self-contained examples you can look through. To run them run `python examples/<example>.py`.

functions.name: examples.simple-logs-of-event.main
documentation: returns all logs of a specific event from a contract within a block range

functions.name: examples.uniswap-pool-swap-events.main
documentation: returns all logs of a the swap event from the uniswap v2 eth/rai swap pool within a block range

functions.name: examples.simple-blocks-and-transactions.main
documentation: returns all block and transaction objects within a block range

functions.name: examples.wallet.address_to_topic
documentation: Convert address to topic for filtering. Padds the address with zeroes.

functions.name: examples.simple-blocks-and-transaction-hashes.main
documentation: returns all blocks and the hashes of the transactions (not entire transaction objects) within a block range

functions.name: examples.simple-logs.main
documentation: returns all logs from a contract within a block range

functions.name: hypersync.preset_query_blocks_and_transactions
documentation:
Returns a query for all Blocks and Transactions within the block range (from_block, to_block]
If to_block is None then query runs to the head of the chain.
Note: this is only for quickstart purposes.  For the best performance, create a custom query
that only includes the fields you'll use in `field_selection`.

functions.name: hypersync.preset_query_blocks_and_transaction_hashes
documentation:
Returns a query object for all Blocks and hashes of the Transactions within the block range
(from_block, to_block].  Also returns the block_hash and block_number fields on each Transaction
so it can be mapped to a block.  If to_block is None then query runs to the head of the chain.
Note: this is only for quickstart purposes.  For the best performance, create a custom query
that only includes the fields you'll use in `field_selection`.

functions.name: hypersync.preset_query_logs
documentation:
Returns a query object for all Logs within the block range (from_block, to_block] from
the given address.  If to_block is None then query runs to the head of the chain.
Note: this is only for quickstart purposes.  For the best performance, create a custom query
that only includes the fields you'll use in `field_selection`.

functions.name: hypersync.preset_query_logs_of_event
documentation:
Returns a query for all Logs within the block range (from_block, to_block] from the
given address with a matching topic0 event signature.  Topic0 is the keccak256 hash
of the event signature.  If to_block is None then query runs to the head of the chain.
Note: this is only for quickstart purposes.  For the best performance, create a custom query
that only includes the fields you'll use in `field_selection`.

classes.name: hypersync.HypersyncClient

methods.name: hypersync.HypersyncClient.__init__
documentation: Creates a new client with the given configuration.

methods.name: hypersync.HypersyncClient.get_height
documentation: Get the height of the hypersync server with retries.

methods.name: hypersync.HypersyncClient.get_chain_id
documentation: Get the chain_id of the hypersync server with retries.

methods.name: hypersync.HypersyncClient.collect
documentation:
Retrieves blocks, transactions, traces, and logs through a stream using the provided
query and stream configuration.

Implementation:
Runs multiple queries simultaneously based on config.concurrency.
Each query runs until it reaches query.to, server height, any max_num_* query param,
or execution timed out by server.

methods.name: hypersync.HypersyncClient.collect_events
documentation: Retrieves events through a stream using the provided query and stream configuration.

methods.name: hypersync.HypersyncClient.collect_arrow
documentation:
Retrieves blocks, transactions, traces, and logs in Arrow format through a stream using
the provided query and stream configuration.

methods.name: hypersync.HypersyncClient.collect_parquet
documentation:
Writes parquet file getting data through a stream using the provided path, query,
and stream configuration.

methods.name: hypersync.HypersyncClient.get
documentation: Executes query with retries and returns the response.

methods.name: hypersync.HypersyncClient.get_events
documentation:
Add block, transaction and log fields selection to the query, executes it with retries
and returns the response.

methods.name: hypersync.HypersyncClient.get_arrow
documentation: Executes query with retries and returns the response in Arrow format.

methods.name: hypersync.HypersyncClient.stream
documentation: Spawns task to execute query and return data via a channel.

methods.name: hypersync.HypersyncClient.stream_events
documentation:
Add block, transaction and log fields selection to the query and spawns task to execute it,
returning data via a channel.

methods.name: hypersync.HypersyncClient.stream_arrow
documentation: Spawns task to execute query and return data via a channel in Arrow format.
Internal client to handle http requests and retries.
